title: Rava Coconut Laddu

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Sugar – 3/4 cup (adjust based on sweetness preference)
Saffron strands (optional) – a few, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk

instruction.title: Step 1: Roast the Rava
steps: Heat 2 tbsp of ghee in a heavy-bottom pan on medium flame.
Add the rava and roast gently, stirring continuously, until it turns light golden and aromatic (~5-6 minutes).
Remove from pan and let it cool slightly.

instruction.title: Step 2: Roast Nuts and Coconut
steps: In the same pan, add 1 tbsp ghee.
Fry cashews until golden and raisins until they puff up. Remove and set aside.
Lightly roast the grated coconut for 2-3 minutes, just enough to reduce moisture but retain softness.

instruction.title: Step 3: Make Sugar Syrup / Mix
steps: Chef’s tip: For a smooth texture, slightly melt sugar in milk instead of dry mixing.
In a pan, combine sugar and milk. Heat gently until sugar dissolves completely.
Add cardamom powder and soaked saffron (if using).
Slowly mix in the roasted rava and coconut, stirring continuously.
Cook on low flame until the mixture thickens and leaves the sides of the pan.

instruction.title: Step 4: Bind the Laddus
steps: Let the mixture cool just enough to handle.
Grease your palms with a little ghee and shape the mixture into smooth, round laddus.
Press a cashew or raisin on top for garnish.

instruction.title: Step 5: Final Touch
steps: Allow laddus to cool completely at room temperature before storing.
Store in an airtight container for up to a week; for longer shelf life, refrigerate.

chefTips: Ghee: Use fresh, aromatic ghee for a rich flavor.
Coconut: Freshly grated coconut is best; desiccated coconut can be used but slightly reduces creaminess.
Rava: Fine, high-quality rava gives smoother laddus.
Binding: Slightly undercooked sugar syrup leads to softer laddus; overcooking makes them hard.
Presentation: Uniform size and smooth finish elevate it to restaurant style.
